title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. lanuginosum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. lanuginosum, also known as woolly bracken, is a subspecies of the Pteridium aquilinum fern plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is known to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. lanuginosum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Pteridium aquilinum subsp. lanuginosum is often dried and used in tea infusions or taken as a tincture, with some studies suggesting its potential as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
